_description: The full messages array sent to LiteLLM after 5 turns. Each turn N sees messages[0 : 2*N].

messages.content:
<lens module_title="Threat Models" lens_title="Risks from AI">
<segment index="1" type="text">
<source>Lens Academy</source>
This lens covers the key risks from advanced AI systems.
</segment>
<segment index="2" type="article-excerpt">
<source>Concrete Problems in AI Safety, by Amodei et al.</source>
The article content goes here.
</segment>
<segment index="3" type="chat">
<source>Chat discussion</source>
</segment>
</lens>
<student-position>Segment 2 of "Risks from AI"</student-position>
<segment-instructions>
Ask the student what they think about the alignment problem.
Do not give away the answer.
</segment-instructions>

What does reward hacking mean?
Reward hacking is when an AI finds unintended ways to maximize its reward.
Can you give me an example?
A classic example is a robot rewarded for moving forward that learns to be very tall and fall over.

<student-position>Segment 3 of "Risks from AI"</student-position>

<segment-instructions>
Help the student explore the key takeaways from this lens.
</segment-instructions>

I think the alignment problem is really about ensuring AI systems do what we actually want.
That's a great intuition. The alignment problem is fundamentally about the gap between what we specify and what we actually want.

<lens module_title="Threat Models" lens_title="What Could Go Wrong?">
<segment index="1" type="text">
<source>Lens Academy</source>
This lens surveys the main threat taxonomies.
</segment>
<segment index="2" type="chat">
<source>Chat discussion</source>
</segment>
</lens>
<student-position>Segment 1 of "What Could Go Wrong?"</student-position>
<segment-instructions>
Help the user learn about AI safety.
</segment-instructions>

How does this relate to what we just read about concrete problems?
Great question. The concrete problems paper focuses on near-term technical issues, while threat taxonomies take a broader view.

<student-position>Segment 2 of "What Could Go Wrong?"</student-position>

<segment-instructions>
Discuss which threat model the student finds most concerning and why.
</segment-instructions>

I find the misuse risks most concerning.
